extraction.one_line_summary: BENNETT Sam timed his sprint perfectly from BORA's leadout, capitalizing on chaos after MARTIN Tony's late attack disrupted VIVIANI's Quick-Step train.
tactical_patterns: MARTIN Tony's late solo attack from 13.6km shattered VIVIANI's leadout train positioning, forcing Quick-Step to chase reactively.
BORA positioned VAN POPPEL Danny through final corner first, creating numerical advantage before BENNETT's final acceleration.
Quick-Step riders physically leaning on VIVIANI under flamme rouge suggests leadout train disorganization under sustained pressure.
SÉNÉCHAL Florian's intermediate sprint work for Quick-Step reveals team prioritizing points jersey accumulation beyond stage wins.

rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: positive: perfectly timed sprint launch demonstrates patience to wait for chaos rather than following standard leadout rhythm
rider_signals.MARTIN Tony: positive: solo 13.6km acceleration shows willingness to sacrifice personal result to disrupt rival sprint trains tactically
rider_signals.VIVIANI Elia: negative: Quick-Step leadout fractured under pressure despite numerical presence, losing organization in final kilometer
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: positive: BORA's corner positioning through final turn superior to Quick-Step's despite latter having more numbers
rider_signals.SÉNÉCHAL Florian: neutral: intermediate sprint targeting reveals Quick-Step building points classification depth beyond VIVIANI alone
